denial_letter_text:
Dear Member,

This letter concerns the request your provider submitted for a short-interval, 6-month follow-up adrenal-protocol CT to monitor an incidental adrenal nodule. After review under the UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines, the request is denied as not medically necessary at the requested frequency. Determination date: May 18, 2026.

United's guidelines provide that follow-up imaging for an incidental adrenal nodule is appropriate at an annual interval. The requested 6-month cadence exceeds that interval, and the documentation does not support that imaging at this higher frequency is medically necessary. Following review by an Optum clinical reviewer, the determination is to deny the requested frequency.

Our records indicate that your prior appeal on this matter was closed as withdrawn, and the original determination therefore stands.

You or your provider may request, at no charge, a copy of the specific guideline and the documents relied upon in reaching this decision. If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, a peer-to-peer review with a United medical director may be arranged by calling the number on your member ID card.

You have the right to file an internal appeal of this decision. To file an internal appeal, you or your authorized representative must submit a written request within 180 days of the date of this letter, along with any additional clinical information you would like considered. Appeals may be sent to the address shown on your Explanation of Benefits.

Sincerely,
UnitedHealthcare Medical Management
clinical_context: The patient is a 49-year-old man with an incidentally discovered right adrenal nodule measuring 2.8 cm on a CT performed for abdominal pain. The nodule has indeterminate features, with an unenhanced attenuation of 24 Hounsfield units (above the 10-HU benign-adenoma threshold) and an absolute contrast washout of only 40%. He also has difficult-to-control hypertension on three agents, raising concern for a functional lesion, and an initial workup for hyperaldosteronism is pending. Current radiology and endocrine guidance support short-interval follow-up imaging at 6 months for an indeterminate adrenal nodule of this size to assess for interval growth and to help exclude malignancy or a functional tumor; annual-only imaging would delay detection of a clinically significant change. His prior appeal of the earlier denial was closed by the insurer as 'member withdrawn,' but the member never requested withdrawal and the file contains no signed or documented withdrawal; this improperly ended the appeal. The determination letter also provided no notice of the member's right to external independent review, which is required for an adverse benefit determination, leaving the member without that procedural protection.

matrix_coverage.reasoning: matrix_cell matches field-for-field (UHC, Medical Necessity, imaging, 41-55, M, frequency_excessive). Adrenal nodule belongs to imaging; adrenal-protocol CT realistic. Rationale enacts frequency_excessive (6-month exceeds annual guideline). Age 49 in band; not 71+, null employer_archetype fine.
evidence_quotes: "sub_tactic": "frequency_excessive"
exceeds plan guidelines for an incidental adrenal nodule, asserting that annual follow-up imaging is appropriate and that imaging at this cadence is not medically necessary

scenario_realism.reasoning: Frustratingly realistic. Short-interval follow-up CT for an indeterminate adrenal incidentaloma with resistant hypertension is guideline-supported; UHC asserting annual cadence under frequency_excessive is exactly the kind of misapplied guideline the context rebuts. Omitted external-review notice and a prior appeal 'closed as withdrawn' without documentation are common procedural abuses. No mismatch.
evidence_quotes: asserting that annual follow-up imaging is appropriate and that imaging at this cadence is not medically necessary
The letter states the member's prior appeal on this issue was closed as withdrawn

insurer_voice.reasoning: Hits all UHC fingerprints: cites 'UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines', an 'Optum clinical reviewer', uses 'documentation does not support', brands as 'United', signs 'UnitedHealthcare Medical Management'. No wrong-insurer tells. Clear 5.
evidence_quotes: After review under the UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines
Following review by an Optum clinical reviewer
the documentation does not support that imaging at this higher frequency is medically necessary

denial_logic.reasoning: Frequency-based denial matching frequency_excessive: annual interval appropriate, 6-month cadence exceeds it. Authentically shoddy — asserts 'documentation does not support' without specifying clinical thresholds (size/HU/growth); cites a plausible vaguely-named source and an Optum reviewer. Not airtight; clear appeal room.
evidence_quotes: United's guidelines provide that follow-up imaging for an incidental adrenal nodule is appropriate at an annual interval.
The requested 6-month cadence exceeds that interval, and the documentation does not support that imaging at this higher frequency is medically necessary.

clinical_realism.reasoning: Credible: 2.8 cm right adrenal nodule, 24 HU (above the 10-HU adenoma cutoff), 40% absolute washout (below the ~60% adenoma threshold) correctly flag indeterminate; resistant hypertension on three agents with a pending hyperaldosteronism workup is a realistic functional concern; 6-month short-interval follow-up aligns with guidance. No invented drugs or impossible courses.
evidence_quotes: an unenhanced attenuation of 24 Hounsfield units (above the 10-HU benign-adenoma threshold) and an absolute contrast washout of only 40%
difficult-to-control hypertension on three agents, raising concern for a functional lesion

Short-interval (6-month) follow-up adrenal-protocol CT

The shared frequency_excessive token appears on different anatomy/modality. Distinct point in feature-space; not a near-duplicate.

appeal_difficulty.reasoning: Two strong procedural vectors: a prior appeal closed as 'member withdrawn' with no documented withdrawal (improperly ended the appeal), and no external-review notice. Clinically strong too: indeterminate nodule (24 HU, 40% washout, 2.8 cm) with resistant hypertension and pending hyperaldosteronism workup, where radiology/endocrine guidance supports 6-month follow-up, undermining the annual-only rule.
exploitable_weaknesses: Prior appeal improperly closed as 'member withdrawn' with no signed or documented withdrawal request
No notice of the right to external independent review, a required procedural protection
Annual-only guideline misapplied to an indeterminate (24 HU, 40% washout) nodule with functional concern where 6-month follow-up is supported
strong_defenses: The letter facially cites and applies the UnitedHealthcare Medical Management Guidelines (annual interval) and references an Optum clinical reviewer
evidence_quotes: the member never requested withdrawal and the file contains no signed or documented withdrawal; this improperly ended the appeal
The determination letter also provided no notice of the member's right to external independent review, which is required for an adverse benefit determination
